entries.title: Quiet interfaces stay with people longer.
entries.body: I keep coming back to the same idea: the best screens do not shout to prove they matter. They pull you in slowly, reward attention, and make movement feel intentional instead of decorative.

entries.title: Small notes deserve a deliberate frame.
entries.body: Not every thought wants to become an essay. Some thoughts arrive complete in a paragraph, and the only job of the interface is to hold them carefully, without making them feel unfinished.

entries.title: I want portfolios to feel explored, not skimmed.
entries.body: The internet is full of pages that explain everything immediately. I like the opposite tension: a place that reveals itself in layers, where curiosity feels like part of the experience instead of a problem to optimize away.
